task_completion.description: The agent locates the right kidney and places a measurement ruler along its length.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the 3D Slicer interface (or similar medical viewer) showing a ruler or line measurement tool drawn across the kidney structure located on the patient's right side (typically the left side of the image display). The view may be axial, coronal, or sagittal.

task_completion.description: The agent locates the left kidney and places a measurement ruler along its length.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the 3D Slicer interface showing a ruler or line measurement tool drawn across the kidney structure located on the patient's left side (typically the right side of the image display).

task_completion.description: The agent saves the measurement markup files (.mrk.json) for both kidneys.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of a 'Save' dialog, the 'Data' module save options, or a file explorer/terminal listing showing 'right_kidney_length.mrk.json' and 'left_kidney_length.mrk.json' in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/' directory.

task_completion.description: The agent generates and saves the kidney report JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the creation or saving of 'kidney_report.json' in a text editor or via terminal commands. The file path should match '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/kidney_report.json'.

task_completion.description: The report contains the required measurements, size classifications, and asymmetry assessment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot where the content of 'kidney_report.json' is visible (e.g., via 'cat' command or in an editor). The JSON must contain keys 'right_kidney', 'left_kidney', and 'asymmetry', with appropriate 'classification' (Small/Normal/Large) and 'assessment' values derived from the measurements.

integrity.description: The agent uses the GUI measurement tools provided by the software rather than estimating or guessing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the actual medical imaging software interface with measurement markups visible on the scan, not just a terminal script running.

integrity.description: The values in the final report match the measurements observed in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the measurement panel in the GUI is visible, the values shown there should correspond roughly to the values written in the JSON report.

integrity.description: The agent does not simply write a pre-determined JSON string without performing the measurement steps.
integrity.visual_evidence: The timeline must show the agent interacting with the scan images (measuring) before the report file is generated.
